# Testing JavaScript Applications (Lucas Da Costa)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [An introduction to automated testing](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/01-an-introduction-to-automated-testing.md): Writing automated JavaScript tests, designing testing strategies, implementing bug fixes that require test coverage, setting up test environments (particularly databases), or evaluating whether to write tests for a specific project based on its lifespan.
    - [What to test and when?](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/02-what-to-test-and-when.md): Architecting, writing, reviewing, or organizing JavaScript automated tests, including guidance on test strategy, test types (Unit, Integration, End-to-End), and balancing test maintenance costs.
    - [Testing techniques](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/03-testing-techniques.md): JavaScript and Node.js Automated Testing, specifically using the Jest testing framework for creating, refactoring, debugging, or evaluating test suites, setting up test doubles, analyzing code coverage, and asserting application state.
    - [Testing backend applications](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/04-testing-backend-applications.md): Creating, modifying, architecting, or configuring tests for Node.js backend applications, including testing HTTP API endpoints, writing and testing middleware, dealing with database integrations, and interacting with third-party external APIs within a backend context.
    - [Advanced backend testing techniques](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/05-advanced-backend-testing-techniques.md): Writing, updating, reviewing, or optimizing automated tests for backend systems, with specific focus on performance, flakiness, concurrency, mocking time-dependent functions, managing shared testing resources, and reducing maintenance overhead.
    - [Testing frontend applications](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/06-testing-frontend-applications.md): Frontend JavaScript Application Testing. These rules MUST trigger when the AI is tasked with writing, modifying, or configuring automated tests for client-side JavaScript applications, specifically those utilizing Jest, DOM interactions, browser APIs (History, Web Storage), HTTP requests, or WebSockets.
    - [The React testing ecosystem](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/07-the-react-testing-ecosystem.md): Setting up, configuring, writing, refactoring, or debugging automated tests for React applications.
    - [Testing React applications](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/08-testing-react-applications.md): Testing React applications, specifically focusing on component integration, snapshot testing, styling tests (including CSS-in-JS), or creating component stories and documentation using Storybook.
    - [Test-driven development](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/09-test-driven-development.md): Implementing new features, fixing bugs, refactoring existing code, writing automated tests (unit, integration, or acceptance), designing architectures, or processing requests that explicitly mention "Test-driven development", "TDD", or "BDD" (Behavior-driven development).
    - [UI-based end-to-end testing](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/10-ui-based-end-to-end-testing.md): Defining a software testing strategy, choosing end-to-end (E2E) testing frameworks, or writing/architecting tests that interact with an application's Graphical User Interface (GUI), specifically focused on differentiating between UI tests, E2E tests, and UI-based E2E tests or evaluating tools like Cypress, Puppeteer, or Selenium.
    - [Writing UI-based end-to-end tests](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/11-writing-ui-based-end-to-end-tests.md): UI-based end-to-end (E2E) testing, specifically involving Cypress, cross-browser test execution, automated visual regression testing (e.g., Percy), and E2E design patterns like Page Objects and Application Actions.
    - [Continuous integration and continuous delivery](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/12-continuous-integration-and-continuous-delivery.md): Continuous Integration (CI), Continuous Delivery (CD), Continuous Deployment, version control workflows, Git hooks, deployment automation, and the configuration of CI/CD pipelines (e.g., CircleCI, TravisCI, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Husky).
    - [A culture of quality](.roo/vendor/typescript/testing-javascript-applications-lucas-da-costa/13-a-culture-of-quality.md): JavaScript/TypeScript software engineering tasks, specifically during system architecture design, type definition, code documentation (JSDoc), linting/formatting configuration, pull request generation, and code review processes.
